title: Assignment-1
text: This page contains my Assignment 1 work.

title: Assignment 1
text:
Introduction
This assignment focuses on decision making with analytics.
The goal of this assignment is to analyze a dataset and apply analytical methods to support decision making.

title: Assignment 1
text:
Problem Description
In this assignment, we investigate a decision-making problem using available data.
The objective is to understand the relationships between variables and draw insights that can support better decisions.

title: Assignment 1
text:
Data
The dataset used in this assignment contains observations related to the problem being analyzed.
The variables represent different characteristics that may influence the decision outcome.

title: Assignment 1
text:
Methodology
The analysis will include:

Data exploration

Descriptive statistics

Visualization

Analytical interpretation

These methods help identify patterns and relationships in the data.

title: Assignment 1
text:
Results
The results of the analysis will provide insights into the factors affecting the decision problem.
Visualizations and summary statistics will be used to support the findings.

title: Assignment 1
text:
Conclusion
This assignment demonstrates how analytical tools can support decision making.
The findings highlight important patterns in the data and provide a foundation for further analysis.

title: About Me
text:
My name is Feyza Ăœnal.
I am currently studying Industrial Engineering.
This website was created as part of the EMU660 course to showcase my assignments and coursework.
My interests include data analysis, statistics, and industrial engineering applications.

title: Assignment-1
text:
Video Summary
The video discusses the relationship between Data Science and Industrial Engineering. The speakers explain how data science techniques can be used to improve decision-making in engineering systems and business processes.
They emphasize that modern organizations generate large amounts of data and industrial engineers can use this data to optimize production systems, logistics, and operations. The talk also highlights the importance of programming, statistics, and analytical thinking for engineers who want to work in data-driven environments.
Overall, the video shows how data science tools help industrial engineers analyze complex systems and make more efficient and informed decisions.

title: Assignment-1
text:
Open-ended Question
How can data science improve decision-making in industrial engineering?
Answer
Data science helps industrial engineers analyze large datasets, identify patterns, and optimize processes. By using statistical analysis and machine learning techniques, engineers can make better predictions and improve operational efficiency.

title: Assignment-1
text:
Multiple Choice Question
Which skill is essential for combining data science with industrial engineering?

Artistic drawing

Statistical analysis

Musical composition

Architecture design

Correct Answer: B) Statistical analysis


# Personal variables
my_first <- "Feyza"
my_birth_year <- 1990

# Calculate k
k <- (nchar(my_first) + my_birth_year) %% 15 + 8
cat("Calculated value of k:", k, "\n")

Calculated value of k: 8 

# Custom statistical summary function
compute_stats <- function(x) {
  list(
    mean = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    variance = var(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr = IQR(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    min = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    max = max(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Using sapply
cat("\n--- sapply results ---\n")


--- sapply results ---

sapply(mtcars, compute_stats)

iqr      7.375    4        205.175  83.5     0.84      1.02875  2.0075  
min      10.4     4        71.1     52       2.76      1.513    14.5    
max      33.9     8        472      335      4.93      5.424    22.9    
         vs        am        gear      carb    
mean     0.4375    0.40625   3.6875    2.8125

iqr      1         1         1         2       
min      0         0         3         1

print(apply_results)

### Task 3-d


library(dslabs)
data("polls_us_election_2016")

data_original <- polls_us_election_2016

# Display k rows
cat("\nRows from original dataset:\n")

  print(head(data_original, k))
} else {
  print(tail(data_original, k))
}

cat("\nTotal NA values:", sum(is.na(data_original)), "\n")


Total NA values: 11604 

# NA per column

cat("\nNA values per column (top 8):\n")


NA values per column (top 8):

print(head(na_per_column, 8))

rawpoll_mcmullin adjpoll_mcmullin  rawpoll_johnson  adjpoll_johnson 
            4178             4178             1409             1409 
           grade       samplesize            state        startdate 
             429                1                0                0 

# Replace NA values
new_data <- data_original

for(i in 1:ncol(new_data)) {

  if(is.numeric(new_data[[i]])) {

    new_data[[i]][is.na(new_data[[i]])] <- my_birth_year + k

  } else {

    new_data[[i]] <- as.character(new_data[[i]])

    new_data[[i]][is.na(new_data[[i]])] <- paste0(my_first, "_", k)

  }

}

# Display rows from new dataset

title: Assignment-1
text:
Custom Summary Function
compute_stats <- function(x){ list( mean = mean(x), median = median(x), variance = var(x), IQR = IQR(x), min = min(x), max = max(x) ) }
